mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, it signals a sudden labor demand shock that triggers corporate earnings revisions downward, depressing S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment crossing signal, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: The joint divergence between housing starts and industrial production captures a structural shift in capital allocation; when housing construction accelerates while factory output stagnates, corporate capex cycles decouple from consumer demand, compressing equity multiples over the following quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term (HOUST growth minus INDPRO growth) shows no statistically significant correlation with forward equity returns (p > 0.10) over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: During active monetary tightening cycles, a rising federal funds rate combined with sticky inflation erodes real corporate profit margins through higher financing costs and input price pass-through, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when both the federal funds rate and CPI index rise simultaneously for three consecutive months, indicating the margin compression mechanism is broken.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly scales forward earnings expectations for S&P 500 constituents; sustained positive GDP growth accelerates revenue recognition and lifts equity valuations within the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth quarters consistently yield negative or flat S&P 500 5-day forward returns, breaking the earnings-growth linkage.

mechanism_narrative: A breakdown in industrial production below the 100 index baseline indicates a severe supply-side contraction that forces inventory drawdowns and margin compression, depressing equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive when industrial production sustains levels below 100 for two consecutive months, disproving the supply contraction valuation penalty.

mechanism_narrative: The ratio of nonfarm payrolls growth to the unemployment rate captures labor market tightness; when payroll expansion outpaces unemployment declines, wage-push inflation accelerates, forcing discount rate repricing and negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The payroll-to-unemployment ratio shows no predictive power for forward equity returns (Rank IC < 0.02) over any 3-year rolling window, or positive ratio expansions coincide with equity rallies.

mechanism_narrative: In housing-led expansion phases, rising residential construction activity crowds out commercial investment and increases mortgage rates, which suppresses consumer discretionary spending and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive when both residential construction starts and mortgage applications rise concurrently, invalidating the crowding-out mechanism.
